items.hook: Robert Peston found it 'too difficult' returning to Wales after his wife's death
items.body: The couple spent a lot of time in Wales, enjoying walks along the coast.

items.hook: Every year 6 student to be given Katherine Rundell book for Christmas
items.body:
More than 800,000 copies of Impossible Creatures will be distributed to year 6 and P6 children through The Queen’s Christmas Present initiative

items.hook: Why a Ukrainian cruise missile is flying with hobby drone hardware
items.body: An open-source flight-control system found in consumer drones has been installed in Ukraine’s latest cruise missile, showing how cheap technology is making military hardware accessible to all

items.hook: The Adventures of Gil Blas is a 1956 French–Spanish adventure film directed by René Jolivet and Ricardo Muñoz Suay and starring Georges Marchal, Barbara Laage and Susana Canales.
items.body: It is based on the eighteenth century novel Gil Blas by Alain-René Lesage. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Alex is a crime novel written by French novelist Pierre Lemaitre.
items.body: The novel was published in French in 2011 and was translated into English by Frank Wynne in 2013. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: Mangalya Balam is a 1959 Indian Telugu-language romantic drama film, produced by D.
items.body: Madhusudhana Rao under Annapurna Pictures banner and directed by Adurthi Subba Rao. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Bruno Mars has an unexpected opening before Wembley party starts
items.body: After an eight-year absence from UK stages, Bruno Mars returned to Wembley Stadium with a polished, hit-filled show.

items.opener: Here's one for the group chat —
items.hook: Swords of Steel is a 1933 children's historical fiction novel written by Elsie Singmaster and illustrated by David Hendrickson.
items.body: Set before and during the American Civil War, it tells of the childhood and coming of age of a boy from the North and his involvement with the war, and was inspired by Singmaster's childhood growing up in Pennsylvania. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Military Philosophers is the ninth of Anthony Powell's twelve-novel sequence A Dance to the Music of Time.
items.body: First published in 1968, it covers the latter part of Nicholas Jenkins' service in World War II. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.
